L2.description: A memory corruption vulnerability exists in the JavaScript engine's parser where internal optimization data structures are incorrectly referenced during formal parameter list processing. When crafted JavaScript containing nested constructs such as arrow functions or function expressions within default parameter values is parsed, an out-of-bounds memory access occurs, enabling potential remote code execution.

L3.area: Function declaration parsing and formal parameter handling within the script parser
L3.description: A memory corruption vulnerability exists in the JavaScript engine's parser where deferred parse optimization data is incorrectly consulted while processing function formal parameter lists. When a function declaration includes default parameter values containing nested arrow functions, function expressions, or accessor definitions, stale internal references lead to out-of-bounds memory access and potential remote code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The parser incorrectly uses deferred function stub data while processing formal argument lists, causing out-of-bounds memory access when arrow functions or nested functions appear within function parameters.
